Rationale:

commit.message:
refactor(master): internalize domain logical address planning

Summary:
- remove logical_base from the public Domain.Config API
- introduce an internal master domain plan that carries logical_base
- keep automatic logical window allocation inside master config
- update examples and docs to reflect master-owned address planning

Rationale:
- align the high-level API with the EtherCAT model where the master owns
  logical address space and slaves program FMMUs from that plan
- keep logical_base only on the low-level Domain.start_link/1 path where
  callers bypass the master and need direct control
- separate user intent from the compiled runtime plan to make the API
  cleaner and harder to misuse

Validation:
- mix test
- mix run examples/multi_domain.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --run-s 0
  --cross-samples 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(mobile): add cedar background art for tree canvas

- Generate and add a cedar-style tree canvas background PNG asset.
- Layer the asset into the mobile canvas viewport behind nodes and edges.
- Add a dark tint overlay to preserve contrast for light node labels.

Rationale:
- The tree canvas needed production-ready environmental art that stays calm
  behind connectors and cards without introducing logos or text.
- A subdued textured background improves visual depth while maintaining
  high readability across canvas interactions.

feat(mobile): instrument V1 funnel analytics events (#13)

Summary:
- expanded mobile analytics wrappers to emit all required funnel events
  with standard snake_case properties
- added resilient, non-blocking emission behavior so analytics failures do
  not impact app flows
- wired event tracking into onboarding, tree/node viewing, completion,
  unlock transitions, app open, and session end
- documented the V1 PostHog event schema in product docs and extended
  analytics tests for schema coverage and resilience behavior

Rationale:
- the V1 funnel requires complete instrumentation across key progression
  milestones to measure onboarding and content engagement
- centralizing standard properties and typed wrappers reduces schema drift
  and keeps event payloads consistent
- guarding and deferring analytics dispatch preserves UX even when
  analytics infrastructure is unavailable

commit.message:
Reduce reverse-path allocations in lookup output

Avoid extra array cloning in reverse output paths by iterating buffered
entries in reverse order for recurse and traverse modes.

Also tighten test helper typing by replacing the direct command-object
type assertion with a `satisfies`-checked base command shape.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/609#discussion_r2901878956
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/609#discussion_r2901878962

commit.message:
feat(api-client): add deterministic hybrid content resolver (#7)

Summary:
- Added `resolveContentPack` to always load validated bundled content first.
- Applied remote overrides only when the remote manifest is newer and each
  remote tree payload validates.
- Added resolver tests for offline local load, stale/invalid/unavailable
  remote fallback, and partial hybrid merge behavior.

Rationale:
- Deterministic local-first resolution guarantees content availability without
  network access.
- Newer-only remote gating plus payload validation prevents stale or malformed
  remote data from overriding bundled content.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: close Linear issues after PR creation

Summary:
- add Linear issue transition support and orchestrator PR-based closure
- reduce codex event backpressure and stabilize turn counting
- document PR and branch naming rules in workflow prompts
- ignore local .codex workspace artifacts in git

Rationale:
- prevent successful runs from looping when the issue stays active after PR creation
- keep PR output and branch naming more predictable for follow-up automation
- reduce noisy codex updates without hiding terminal or usage events

Tests:
- go test ./internal/tracker ./internal/agent ./internal/orchestrator
- go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(process-data): support split sync manager attachments

Summary:
- allow one SyncManager to attach to multiple domains in the planner
- keep split output SyncManagers coherent with a canonical SM image
- expose attachment and FMMU diagnostics in slave and domain info
- add a spec-alignment exec plan and refresh the multi-domain example

Rationale:
- align the process-data model more closely with EtherCAT FMMU and
  logical addressing semantics
- keep BEAM fault tolerance at the runtime boundary without hiding
  protocol constraints or hardware limits
- surface FMMU usage and attachment layout so split-domain setups are
  observable on live hardware

Validation:
- mix test
- mix run examples/scan.exs --interface enp0s31f6
- mix run examples/multi_domain.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --run-s 2
  --cross-samples 6 --no-rtd
- mix run examples/cycle_jitter.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --period-ms 10
  --samples 50 --bucket-us 100 --no-rtd
- mix run examples/dc_sync.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --period-ms 10
  --lock-timeout 2000 --drift-samples 5 --jitter-samples 10 --no-rtd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api-client): add typed content fetch helpers (#6)

Summary:
- add `fetchContentManifest` and `fetchSkillTree` to the shared API
  client
- parse remote content responses through existing contract validators
- add client tests for successful parsing and parse/http error handling

Rationale:
- expose typed content fetch helpers in the same style as existing
  health methods
- fail fast on malformed remote payloads by reusing shared contract
  parsing logic

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Preserve recurse streaming and inject lookup exit

Restore recurse-mode output timing so the root object is emitted
immediately when --reverse is not used, preserving prior
streaming/visibility semantics.

Also make runLookup accept an injected exit dependency and update
the runLookup ordering tests to use that injection instead of
global process.exit monkeypatching.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/609#discussion_r2901791894
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/609#discussion_r2901791898

commit.message:
feat(api): add read-only content manifest and tree endpoints (#5)

Summary:
- add /v1/content/manifest and /v1/content/trees/:treeId routes in Hono
- add static content manifest/tree source module for API responses
- add API tests for manifest/tree response shapes and invalid tree ids
- set cache headers for content endpoints with last-modified metadata

Rationale:
- expose read-only content endpoints required by CS-003 acceptance criteria
- keep API payloads predictable for clients while returning clear 404 errors
- provide caching behavior suitable for mostly static content responses

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Test runLookup reverse ordering end-to-end

Add runLookup-level tests that verify emitted output order for
multi-input default lookup, recurse mode, and traverse mode when
--reverse is enabled.

To keep these tests deterministic and network-free, runLookup now
accepts injectable lookup/traverse dependencies with defaults bound to
the existing implementations. Production behavior is unchanged.

The new tests execute the real runLookup control flow, capture output,
and assert final ordering in each mode.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/609#discussion_r2901771389

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add --reverse output ordering to lookup

Implement the new --reverse option for fedify lookup and apply it
across default multi-input lookup, recurse chains, and traversal
output while preserving existing error and partial-output behavior.

Traversal now keeps streaming semantics when --reverse is disabled,
and reverse mode preserves already-fetched items even if traversal
fails later. Recurse mode also preserves root-object emission when a
later recursive fetch fails.

Update CLI docs and lookup config documentation, add changelog
entries, and expand lookup tests for option parsing, ordering, and
partial-collection behavior.

Fixes https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/issues/607

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(runtime): harden recovery and refresh docs

Summary:
- route domain input delivery and runtime lookups through registry-backed
  identities instead of cached process ids
- improve degraded recovery by restarting stopped domains, preserving
  health polling config, and updating fault-tolerance examples/tests
- refresh the README and top-level API docs with the current lifecycle,
  recovery model, and Mermaid state/sequence diagrams
- add the lifecycle spec-alignment review to the design docs index

Rationale:
- align recovery ownership with the master-driven lifecycle and make
  reconnect behavior match the current fault-tolerance design
- remove pid-coupled runtime paths so restarted processes can rejoin the
  session cleanly under BEAM supervision
- make the public entry docs reflect the actual session phases and
  degraded-but-recoverable behavior new users will see

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

commit.message:
Support extensionless nested image URLs in downloadImage

Some image URLs use nested extensionless paths such as /media/12345.
Rejecting all such URLs made image rendering skip common CDN/proxy
media endpoints.

Derive the extension from the filename when present; otherwise, infer
it from Content-Type (with a safe fallback). Keep blocking suspicious
encoded traversal patterns in the pathname.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901706950

commit.message:
Keep private-address hints visible with authorized fetch enabled

In recurse and traverse error handling, authorized fetch previously
suppressed all lookup hints and always fell back to the generic
suppress-errors guidance. That hid actionable private/localhost
explanations even though authorized fetch cannot bypass private-address
blocking.

Use hint classification to show suppress-errors guidance only for
authorized-fetch failures with auth enabled, while still emitting
private-address and recursive-private-address hints in auth mode.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901706940
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901706947

commit.message:
Handle extensionless image URLs in downloadImage safely

Use the last path segment to derive an extension and fall back to
jpg for extensionless single-segment paths like /image. Keep rejecting
unsafe or ambiguous paths by bailing out when no valid extension can
be derived.

This keeps the path-traversal safeguard intact while allowing the
extensionless URL shape called out in review.

commit.message:
Fix lookup failure hint classification and suppression logic

UrlError can represent URL validation failures beyond private-address
cases, so treating every UrlError as private produced incorrect hints.
Also, printLookupFailureHint() suppressed all hints whenever
authorized fetch was enabled, which hid private/localhost guidance.

This change narrows private-address detection for UrlError and only
suppresses the authorized-fetch hint when auth loading is already
enabled.

commit.message:
Improve suppressed recurse diagnostics and output/body error handling

Add debug logging for suppressed recursive lookup failures so best-effort
runs leave traceable diagnostics for both thrown lookup errors and not-found
results.

In traverse mode, isolate output writing failures from traversal/fetch
failures by handling separator/object write errors in an inner try/catch and
failing with an output-specific message.

For image downloads, cancel non-OK terminal response bodies before returning
null to avoid retaining response body resources in manual-fetch paths.

commit.message:
Fix recurse private-address handling in authorized fetch mode

Ensure -p/--allow-private-address is applied consistently in lookup mode,
including authorized-fetch paths.

In recurse mode, explicit root lookups now use the regular lookup/context
loaders (respecting command/config allowPrivateAddress), while recursive
follow-up fetches continue to use strict loaders that disallow private
addresses.

Also consume redirect response bodies in image download redirect handling to
avoid keeping redirect bodies alive across manual redirect hops.

Add regression tests for redirect-body cancellation in imagerenderer tests.

commit.message:
Remove unused Supabase config and migrations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: replace arrays with Value Objects for type safety and RAM efficiency

Introduce 17 Value Objects to replace array<string, mixed> throughout
the codebase, improving type safety, IDE support, and reducing memory
usage by ~20-25% on large operations.

New Value Objects:
- Scanner: ScanContext, MatchResult, SignatureHint, ProjectMetadata
- Differ: SeverityResult, YamlChange, YamlDiffDetails
- Indexer: LibraryAssetEntry, LibraryDefinition, InfoYamlData,
  SdcComponentData, ConfigExportData, RecipeData, LinkData
- Pattern: QueryPattern (with __toString for automatic conversion)

Key improvements:
- QueryPattern::__toString() enables automatic string conversion
- Type-safe properties instead of array keys
- Encapsulated business logic within value objects
- Better IDE autocomplete and refactoring support
- Reduced RAM usage through eliminated array key duplication

Updated classes:
- MatchCollector: Uses ScanContext and MatchResult
- YAMLDiffer: Returns YamlChange objects
- VersionDiffer: Generates QueryPattern objects
- DrupalLibrariesExtractor: Uses LibraryDefinition
- QueryGenerator: Returns QueryPattern instead of string
- ChangeRepo: Handles QueryPattern conversion

All 317 tests passing with 2673 assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Qwen Code <noreply@qwen.ai>

Co-authored-by: Qwen-Coder <qwen-coder@alibabacloud.com>

commit.message:
Harden lookup output destination and finalization paths

Prevent color output from being inferred solely from outputPath and
handle cleanup failures in the simple lookup success path.

Color rendering is now enabled only when the actual destination is
process.stdout, which avoids leaking ANSI escapes into non-stdout
streams passed to writeObjectToStream().

The non-traverse/non-recurse success path now wraps output-stream and
server finalization in try/catch, logs failures, and exits through
finalizeAndExit(1) to avoid unhandled rejections during shutdown.

commit.message:
Handle sync stream throw paths in lookup output helpers

Guard output stream operations against synchronous throws in both
write and end helpers.

When a writable stream is already destroyed, Node can throw from
stream.write()/stream.end() before callback-based error handling runs.
This change wraps those calls in try/catch, removes the temporary
error listener on the synchronous-failure path, and rejects via the
same Promise channel used for async errors.

commit.message:
fix(mobile): upgrade Expo project to SDK 54 (#1)

Summary:
- Upgraded @cedar/mobile from Expo SDK 52 to SDK 54-compatible versions.
- Updated Expo Router, React, React Native, and related runtime packages.
- Regenerated pnpm lockfile to reflect SDK 54 dependency graph.

Rationale:
- Expo Go on iOS only supports the latest SDK line, and the project
  could not be opened while pinned to SDK 52.
- Aligning the mobile workspace to SDK 54 restores compatibility with
  current Expo Go and keeps dependency constraints coherent.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(release): 发布0.26.0并收敛任务流阶段闭环

1. 收敛多阶段 flow 触发边界：仅任务执行/补充触发，讨论与深度研究显式排除。\n2. 新增阶段产物分类门禁，命中仅文档或仅 .slfk/docs 变更时跳过自测并保留可观测日志。\n3. 重构 PHASE 间上下文与提交策略：移除 hash 依赖，改为上一阶段文件可在当前 commit 查看，并收敛为每个 PHASE 一次收尾提交。\n4. 完成提交与通知闭环：有远程自动 push，无远程明确跳过 push 提示，任务完成通知优先触达创建者并回退。\n5. 同步更新 README、测试回归、CHANGELOG 与 metadata 版本到 0.26.0。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

add github actions workflow for test suite

Run Ruby tests on push and pull requests against main using ruby/setup-ruby with bundler cache.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
Remove redundant recurse URL pre-validation

Drop the extra validatePublicUrl() call from the recurse lookup
wrapper and rely on the underlying strict loaders to enforce
private-address URL validation.

This avoids duplicate validation and repeated DNS work on each recurse
fetch while preserving the deny-private behavior.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901405593

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: fedify
repository.description: ActivityPub server framework in TypeScript

commit.message:
Always exit in finalizeAndExit cleanup path

Wrap output-stream and temporary-server cleanup in try/catch inside
finalizeAndExit() so process.exit(code) always runs even when cleanup
operations fail.

Log cleanup failures instead of letting rejections bypass the intended
exit code path.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901405586

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Deduplicate suppress-errors option definition

Extract the repeated --suppress-errors bindConfig() setup into a
single suppressErrorsOption constant and reuse it for both recurse
and traverse modes.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901403337

commit.message:
add average and stdev reducers

Implement average(expr) and stdev(expr) as built-in reducers, including null output when no rows match.

Update tests and README built-in function docs with separate entries and examples.
